# @fluenti/solid

> SolidJS bindings for Fluenti — compile-time i18n with signals-based reactivity.

## Overview

@fluenti/solid provides SolidJS components, hooks, and context for internationalization. It uses SolidJS signals for reactive locale switching — any computation that calls `t()` will automatically re-run when the locale changes. Built on @fluenti/core.

- Package: @fluenti/solid
- Docs: https://fluenti.dev
- Repository: https://github.com/usefluenti/fluenti/tree/main/packages/solid
- Peer dependency: solid-js ^1.8
- Dependency: @fluenti/core
- License: MIT

## Installation

```bash
pnpm add @fluenti/core @fluenti/solid @fluenti/vite-plugin
```

## Exports

### createI18n(config)

Initialize the global i18n singleton. Call once at app startup before any `useI18n()`. Signals are created inside a `createRoot` so they outlive any component scope.

In browser environments, sets the global singleton automatically. In SSR, logs a warning recommending `<I18nProvider>` for per-request isolation (but still sets the global as a fallback).

Config accepts FluentConfig or I18nConfig (extended):
- locale (string, required): Initial locale
- fallbackLocale (string, optional): Fallback when translation missing
- messages (Record<string, Messages>, optional): Static message catalogs
- missing ((locale, id) => string | undefined, optional): Missing key handler

I18nConfig extensions:
- chunkLoader ((locale: string) => Promise<Record<string, CompiledMessage>>, optional): Async chunk loader for code-splitting
- splitting (boolean, optional): Enable code-splitting mode
- dateFormats (DateFormatOptions, optional): Named date format styles
- numberFormats (NumberFormatOptions, optional): Named number format styles

Returns I18nContext.

### createI18nContext(config)

Create a reactive i18n context backed by Solid signals. Unlike `createI18n()`, does not wrap in `createRoot` and does not set the global singleton. Used internally by I18nProvider, and useful for per-component contexts.

Accepts same config as createI18n(). Returns I18nContext.

### I18nProvider

Component providing i18n context to the component tree. Also registers the context as the global singleton so `useI18n()` works in child components rendered outside the tree (e.g. portals).

Props: Same as I18nConfig (locale, fallbackLocale, messages, chunkLoader, splitting, dateFormats, numberFormats, missing).

```tsx
<I18nProvider locale="en" fallbackLocale="en" messages={{ en, ja }}>
  <App />
</I18nProvider>
```

Or with a pre-created context:

```tsx
const i18n = createI18n({ locale: 'en', messages: { en } })
render(() => (
  <I18nProvider i18n={i18n}>
    <App />
  </I18nProvider>
), root)
```

### useI18n()

Access the i18n context. Resolution order:
1. Module-level singleton created by `createI18n()`
2. Nearest `<I18nProvider>` in the component tree

Throws if neither is available.

Returns I18nContext:
- locale(): Reactive accessor (Solid signal) for current locale
- setLocale(locale: string): Promise<void> — Set active locale. Synchronous when splitting is disabled; async when splitting is enabled and the locale hasn't been loaded yet.
- t(id: string | MessageDescriptor, values?: Record<string, unknown>): string — Translate a message. Reads the locale() signal for reactivity. Supports both string IDs and MessageDescriptor objects with id/message fields.
- d(value: Date | number, style?: string): string — Format a date using Intl.DateTimeFormat via @fluenti/core's formatDate
- n(value: number, style?: string): string — Format a number using Intl.NumberFormat via @fluenti/core's formatNumber
- format(message: string, values?: Record<string, unknown>): string — Format an ICU message string directly (no catalog lookup)
- tRaw(message, values?) — Deprecated, use format() instead
- loadMessages(locale: string, messages: Messages): void — Merge additional messages into a locale catalog at runtime
- getLocales(): string[] — Return all locale codes that have loaded messages
- isLoading: Accessor<boolean> — Whether a locale chunk is currently being loaded
- loadedLocales: Accessor<Set<string>> — Set of locales whose messages have been loaded
- preloadLocale(locale: string): void — Preload a locale in the background without switching to it. Silent failure on error.

### Components

**Trans**: Rich text with JSX element interpolation.
```tsx
<Trans>Read the <a href="/docs">documentation</a></Trans>
```

**Plural**: Locale-aware pluralization. Supports both string props and rich text via JSX element props.
```tsx
<Plural value={count} zero="No items" one="1 item" other="{count} items" />

<Plural
  value={count}
  zero={<>No <strong>items</strong> left</>}
  one={<><em>1</em> item remaining</>}
  other={<><strong>{count}</strong> items remaining</>}
/>
```

**Select**: Categorical value selection (gender, role, etc.). Supports string props and rich text via options object.
```tsx
<Select value={gender} male="He" female="She" other="They" />

<Select
  value={gender}
  options={{
    male: <><strong>He</strong> liked this</>,
    female: <><strong>She</strong> liked this</>,
  }}
  other={<><em>They</em> liked this</>}
/>
```

**DateTime**: Date formatting component.

**NumberFormat**: Number formatting component.

### msg

Lazy message descriptor (re-exported from @fluenti/core). For use outside the component tree where auto-injection cannot work.

### Exported Types

- I18nContext: The reactive context interface
- I18nConfig: Extended config with splitting/chunk loader support
- TransProps
- PluralProps
- SelectProps

## Vite Plugin Setup

```ts
// vite.config.ts
import solidPlugin from 'vite-plugin-solid'
import fluenti from '@fluenti/vite-plugin'

export default {
  plugins: [solidPlugin(), fluenti({ framework: 'solid' })],
}
```

## Full Example

```tsx
// index.tsx
import { render } from 'solid-js/web'
import { I18nProvider, createI18n } from '@fluenti/solid'
import App from './App'

const i18n = createI18n({
  locale: 'en',
  fallbackLocale: 'en',
  messages: { en, ja },
})

render(
  () => (
    <I18nProvider i18n={i18n}>
      <App />
    </I18nProvider>
  ),
  document.getElementById('root')!,
)
```

```tsx
// App.tsx
import { useI18n, Trans, Plural, Select } from '@fluenti/solid'

function App() {
  const { t, d, n, locale, setLocale } = useI18n()

  return (
    <div>
      <p>{t('Hello, {name}!', { name: 'World' })}</p>
      <p>{d(new Date(), 'long')}</p>
      <p>{n(1234.5, 'currency')}</p>

      <Trans>Read the <a href="/docs">documentation</a></Trans>

      <Plural value={5} one="# item" other="# items" />

      <Select value="male" male="He" female="She" other="They" />

      <button onClick={() => setLocale('ja')}>Japanese</button>
    </div>
  )
}
```

## Code-Splitting Support

@fluenti/solid supports lazy-loading locale chunks:

```tsx
const i18n = createI18n({
  locale: 'en',
  messages: { en },
  splitting: true,
  chunkLoader: (locale) => import(`./locales/compiled/${locale}.ts`).then(m => m.default),
})
```

When `splitting` is enabled:
- `setLocale()` becomes async: it loads the chunk before switching
- `isLoading` signal reflects the loading state
- `preloadLocale()` can prefetch locales in the background
- `loadedLocales` tracks which locales are available

## Internal Details

- `getGlobalI18nContext()` and `setGlobalI18nContext()` are internal functions used by useI18n and I18nProvider to manage the module-level singleton
- `resetGlobalI18nContext()` is an internal function for testing only
- The `I18nCtx` Solid context object is exported for advanced use cases
- Translation resolution falls back to the fallbackLocale, then the missing handler, then returns the message ID as-is
- The `t()` function reads the `locale()` signal, making all translations reactive — when locale changes via `setLocale()`, all components using `t()` automatically re-render
